title: Abu Dhabi doctors conduct UAE’s first paediatric cardiac biopsy at SKMC - Arabian Business: Latest News on
summary: The first-of-its-kind procedure, performed on a four-year-old, is part of the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi’s Visiting Physicians Programme
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Doctors in Abu Dhabi’s SEHA Sheikh Khalifa Medical City (SKMC) performed a first-of-its-kind paediatric cardiac biopsy, aimed at evaluating cardiac status following heart transplant surgery.

A four-year-old patient who received a heart transplant abroad in 2023 underwent a cardiac biopsy at SKMC. This was carried out by Dr Rizwan Rehman, Consultant Paediatric and Interventional Cardiologist at SKMC, together with Dr Joshua Philip Kanter, Consultant Interventional Cardiologist at Children’s National Hospital, USA.

This procedure was conducted as part of the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi’s (DoH) Visiting Physicians Programme, which brings internationally renowned specialists to Abu Dhabi and enables advanced treatments to be delivered locally. It represents a substantial advancement in paediatric care and contributes to the emirate’s continuous aim to deliver best-in-class care to community members.

Dr Rehman said: “Performing a cardiac biopsy on a paediatric heart transplant patient here at SKMC is a major milestone for our team and for paediatric care in the UAE. This procedure reflects our growing capabilities in managing complex transplant cases.

“With all necessary diagnostic blood tests and cardiac biopsy assessments now available locally, we are significantly improving the continuum of care for our young patients.

“This success also highlights SKMC’s evolution as a Centre of Excellence in paediatric cardiac surgery and our commitment to working with global experts to bring best-in-class healthcare to our community. For children like the four-year-old patient, being able to undergo follow-up procedures in Abu Dhabi means less disruption and more comfort, surrounded by family in a familiar environment.”

Dr Kanter added: “This cardiac biopsy marks a significant milestone for Sheikh Khalifa Medical City and for paediatric transplant care in the UAE. This is the first time SKMC has successfully conducted the procedure for a paediatric heart transplant patient who had previously received her transplant and follow-up care abroad.”

Dr Khuloud Abdullah, Executive Director of International Patient Care in the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi (DoH), commented: “This achievement is a testament to our commitment to advancing healthcare in Abu Dhabi across all specialities and disciplines.

“The successful cardiac biopsy exemplifies the effectiveness of the Visiting Physician Programme in providing access to world-class medical expertise and advancing capabilities locally. By bringing cutting-edge treatments closer to home, we are reinforcing our dedication to ensuring that every child receives the best possible care, fostering a healthier future for our community.”

The accomplishment reflects the vital role played by the Department of Health – Abu Dhabi’s Visiting Physicians Programme in fostering knowledge exchange, strengthening local healthcare capabilities and enabling access to specialised, advanced care within the UAE.

Since the beginning of 2025, the emirate has welcomed 47 visiting specialists and esteemed international experts as part of the DoH Visiting Physician Programme, who have conducted 270 advanced surgical procedures and provided consultations and treatment to 2,200 patients with rare and complex medical cases.
